raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The robustness claim and the benchmark conclusion ('comparable to or somewhat better') contain logical gaps; evaluating whether the reasoning holds is a natural reading of open-ended feedback on this text.","R1.3":"Multiple claims lack quantitative grounding: no precision/recall figures for the benchmark, no depth ranges for the robustness assertion, and no specifics on which alternatives were compared.","R1.4":"The pipeline is described as a 'standard ensemble approach' guided by prior experience, making the novelty delta versus nearest prior work unclear and a likely target of 'what do you think' scrutiny.","R1.6":"The prose is dense, repetitive in sentence structure, and uses hedged vague language throughout ('somewhat better', 'appears to be', 'relatively robust'); writing polish is plausibly within scope.","R1.7":"'Previous studies', 'existing annotation tools', and 'some of the alternatives' are all unattributed; citation coverage is a natural concern when reviewing a methods description."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review focus primarily on scientific rigor and evidential support of the claims (making R1.2, R1.3, R1.4 load-bearing), or primarily on writing quality and prose clarity (making R1.6 load-bearing), or both equally?"}

rationale.R1.2: The robustness claim and the benchmark conclusion ('comparable to or somewhat better') contain logical gaps; evaluating whether the reasoning holds is a natural reading of open-ended feedback on this text.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple claims lack quantitative grounding: no precision/recall figures for the benchmark, no depth ranges for the robustness assertion, and no specifics on which alternatives were compared.
rationale.R1.4: The pipeline is described as a 'standard ensemble approach' guided by prior experience, making the novelty delta versus nearest prior work unclear and a likely target of 'what do you think' scrutiny.
rationale.R1.6: The prose is dense, repetitive in sentence structure, and uses hedged vague language throughout ('somewhat better', 'appears to be', 'relatively robust'); writing polish is plausibly within scope.
rationale.R1.7: 'Previous studies', 'existing annotation tools', and 'some of the alternatives' are all unattributed; citation coverage is a natural concern when reviewing a methods description.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus primarily on scientific rigor and evidential support of the claims (making R1.2, R1.3, R1.4 load-bearing), or primarily on writing quality and prose clarity (making R1.6 load-bearing), or both equally?
